title: Exact solution and pair correlation functions for a generalized three-chain Ising tube with multispin interactions
abstract: We obtain an exact solution for a generalized three-chain Ising tube (TCGIT) of length $L$ with toroidal boundary conditions and the most general $C_3$-invariant Hamiltonian on an elementary prism, containing 20 independent coupling constants, including an external magnetic field. Using an $8\times 8$ transfer matrix, we derive the exact partition function of the finite system and obtain the free energy, internal energy, specific heat, magnetization, magnetic susceptibility, and entropy in the thermodynamic limit $L\to\infty$. In the general case, $λ_{\max}$ is determined by a quartic equation, whereas in the principal special case with even-spin interactions (PSC) the spectrum simplifies substantially: the characteristic polynomial factorizes, and $λ_{\max}$ is given by the root of a quadratic equation. For mirror-symmetric subfamilies, we derive explicit formulas for the pair correlation functions and express the magnetization in terms of the components of the eigenvector associated with $λ_{\max}$; in the even-spin case with $h=0$, the magnetization vanishes. Important special cases include the width-three planar model with nearest-neighbor, next-nearest-neighbor, and plaquette interactions, including the entropy limit $S(T\to0^+)=(\ln 2)/3$ for $k\ge 0$ and $S(T\to0^+)=0$ for $k<0$, as well as the width-three planar triangular model with distinct nearest-neighbor couplings, three-spin interactions involving neighboring triangles, and an external field.
summary: This paper provides an exact analytical solution for a complex 1D-like Ising tube with various multi-spin interactions. It characterizes the thermodynamic properties and phase transitions of the system, specifically highlighting interesting residual entropy behaviors in the gonihedric limit.
main_problem: Obtaining an exact solution and pair correlation functions for a generalized three-chain Ising tube (TCGIT) with multispin interactions.
main_result: The authors derive the exact partition function, thermodynamic quantities (free energy, magnetization, etc.), and pair correlation functions, including identifying a residual entropy in the gonihedric limit.
method: The transfer-matrix formalism is used with an 8x8 matrix, employing symmetry exploitation, Ferrari's method for quartic equations, and the Perron-Frobenius theorem.
model_or_system: A quasi-one-dimensional three-chain Ising tube with toroidal boundary conditions, featuring a C3-invariant Hamiltonian with 20 independent coupling constants and multispin interactions.
key_observables: Partition function, free energy, internal energy, specific heat, magnetization, magnetic susceptibility, entropy, and pair correlation functions.
important_parameters: Temperature (T), external magnetic field (h), and 20 independent coupling constants; includes thermodynamic and low-temperature limits.
main_assumptions: The Hamiltonian is restricted to C3-invariant interactions and the system assumes toroidal boundary conditions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the lattice structure; Figure 2 shows the unfolded planar lattice; Figures 3 and 4 plot thermodynamic quantities and entropy for the gonihedric model; Figures 7-12 map out ground-state phase diagrams and correlation length behavior.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the TCGIT model, applies the transfer-matrix method and symmetry reductions, derives exact thermodynamic quantities and correlation functions, analyzes special cases like the gonihedric and triangular models, and concludes with a detailed ground-state phase diagram analysis.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Three-chain lattice of the Ising tube.
FIG. 2. Planar Ising model with nearest-neighbor, next- nearest-neighbor, and plaquette interactions obtained by un- folding the three-chain Ising tube into the plane.

FIG. 3. Planar model with nearest-neighbor, next-nearest-neighbor, and plaquette interactions. Plots of free energy (a), internal energy (b), specific heat (c) and entropy (d) for the case of Jpl 1 = Jpl 2 = 1
FIG. 4. Planar gonihedric model. Plots of free energy (a), internal energy (b), specific heat (c) and entropy (d) of the gonihedric model at k ∈[−3, 3], T ∈[0.1, 4].

FIG. 6. Planar triangular model. Plots of the free energy (a), internal energy (b), specific heat (c), selected cross sections of the specific heat (d), magnetization (e), selected cross sections of the susceptibility (f), and entropy (g) for the triangular model with J∆ 1 = −0.1, J∆ 2 = −0.11, J∆ 4 = −0.12, J∆ 5 = −0.13, h = 0.15, J∆ 3 = r, T ∈[0.1, 4].
